title: arctostaphylos viscida subsp. viscida
intro: Arctostaphylos viscida subsp. viscida, also known as the white man's foot or bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, and its medicinal properties include its high content of arbutin, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Arctostystaphylos viscida subsp. viscida include its ability to help treat urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other conditions, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support kidney and urinary health, and to promote overall well-being.
